parentQuestion: Which statement correctly distinguishes ANS from somatic nervous system control?

scaffolds.q: Cardiac muscle can generate its own electrical impulses without nervous input.
scaffolds.explain: The heart has its own pacemaker cells — the sinoatrial node fires rhythmically without any neural input. Welcome this realization:this: your heart would beat even if every nerve to it were severed. The ANS modulates the rate, but doesn't initiate the beat. this is beautiful autonomy within autonomic control.

scaffolds.q: Skeletal muscle requires nerve stimulation to contract.
scaffolds.explain: Without motor neuron input, skeletal muscle is paralyzed. Enter this realm: into this stark dependency: skeletal muscle fibers don't generate their own action potentials. They wait, silent and flaccid, until a motor neuron commands them. Denervation means paralysis and eventual atrophy. The muscle withers without its nerve.
